documentation_impact.rationale: Phase 4 dogfood feature-delivery implement stage. The coder authored the timestamp-naming compliance descriptor, three per-feature Spec Contracts, the migration script (tools/migrate-timestamp-naming.mjs) with helper-level tests under node --test, the dry-run manifest fixture, and the inbox-lifecycle handbook §3c rule for actor-owned timestamp prefixing of non-conforming inbox artifacts. ADR 0005 was promoted from work/ draft to memory/adr/0005-timestamp-naming-conventions.md with status accepted. Migration write-mode is intentionally deferred to a post-supervisor task and gated behind TESSERACT_MIGRATION_GO=1 to avoid relocating the in-flight feature's own work directory mid-implement.

deferred_items.rationale: Migration write-mode (--write under TESSERACT_MIGRATION_GO=1) is intentionally deferred to a post-supervisor task that runs from a quiescent state. Renaming the in-flight feature's own work directory during implement would relocate the very files the agent is editing. Plan and ADR document the split.
New domain nouns (collision counter, migration manifest) remain feature-local until the librarian indexing stage decides on glossary promotion.

policy_alignment.agents_md_alignment: Slice delegates the implement stage to the named coder persona per §4, stays inside the touch-set declared at work/timestamp-naming-conventions/touch-set.json (with the four parent-ratified additions documented in this artifact's deferred_items rationale), and stages diffs locally without commit or push.
policy_alignment.constitution_alignment: Slice keeps every emitted artifact dual-anchored, encodes the convention as policy and Spec Contracts BEFORE any rename mutation, and gates write-mode migration behind an explicit env var to preserve replayability and rollback.
policy_alignment.prd_alignment: Slice satisfies the Phase 4 implement-stage requirement (encoded policy + Spec Contracts + migration tool + handbook update + ADR) without expanding scope past the declared touch set; the dry-run manifest empirically validates the migration plan against the live repo state.
